question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the gap-fill path by crafting a subscription whose live listener delivers a message with a sequence number far ahead of the last seen, causing topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() to backfill an attacker-controlled range and present a reordered view of topic history to all co-subscribers sharing the same stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a startTime set to the distant past and no endTime, causing the historical Flux to replay the entire topic message history in a sequence that interleaves with live messages, effectively reorganizing the apparent order of events seen by downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the numMissingMessages calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) by racing two concurrent subscriptions so that the last reference is stale, causing the gap-fill query to retrieve a different range than intended and deliver messages out of their original consensus order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an IllegalStateException by triggering out-of-order message delivery through the safety check Flux racing with the live Flux, resulting in the subscriber stream terminating abruptly and the client receiving an incomplete or reordered history segment?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value only slightly greater than startTime so that the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) boundary cuts the historical stream mid-sequence, delivering a partial and misleading view of the topic's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an endTime that is less than startTime (bypassing isValidEndTime if validation is skipped) so that isComplete() immediately returns true, causing the subscription to terminate before any historical messages are delivered and making the topic history appear empty?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a very large limit value so that the limit arithmetic (filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get()) overflows or produces an unexpected newFilter, causing the live listener to re-query historical ranges and deliver duplicate messages that distort the apparent sequence of topic events?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the duplicate-message suppression branch (numMissingMessages <= -1) by timing two concurrent subscriptions so that a message already processed is re-emitted by the safety check Flux, causing the filter branch to silently drop it and create an apparent gap in the delivered history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of simultaneous subscriptions to the same topic, incrementing subscriberCount without bound, causing resource exhaustion that delays or reorders message delivery for all other subscribers and distorting the apparent consensus ordering of topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the checkTopicExists=false configuration path where a synthetic Entity with type TOPIC is returned for any topicId, allowing subscription to non-existent or deleted topics and receiving fabricated or stale history that misrepresents the actual transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription where the live listener emits a message with a sequence number exactly 2 ahead of last, causing missingMessages() to issue a gap-fill query with limit=1 and endTime=current.getConsensusTimestamp(), and if the retriever returns zero results, the current message is still appended via concatWithValues(current), silently skipping the gap and reorganizing the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value just beyond the current time so that pastEndTime() fires after grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval() (default 30s), and during that window the safety check Flux triggers a gap-fill that retrieves messages beyond the intended endTime boundary, delivering extra history that should not be visible?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) combination where both the safety check and the live listener emit the same message concurrently, causing the downstream filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) to pass the duplicate through before last is updated, resulting in duplicate messages in the delivered history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the safety check Flux (subscribeOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic())) and the live Flux updating the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last, causing isNext() to return true for a message that is actually out of order, silently reorganizing the delivered sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with limit=1 so that after the first live message is processed, the newFilter built in incomingMessages() has limit=0, which hasLimit() treats as no limit, causing the listener to continue streaming all subsequent messages beyond the intended boundary and delivering an unbounded history view?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the safety check path (current == null) by causing the importer to appear stalled (e.g., flooding the system so no new messages arrive within 1 second), triggering repeated gap-fill queries that each re-retrieve the same historical range and deliver duplicate messages, distorting the apparent message count and order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to the minimum valid value (0 or near-zero nanoseconds) to force the historical retriever to scan the entire topic history from genesis, and by combining this with a carefully chosen endTime, selectively expose only a subset of the history that omits certain consensus events, reorganizing what the client perceives as the complete record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last to be set to a message with an artificially high sequence number by exploiting a race between the safety check Flux and the live Flux, so that subsequent calls to isNext() reject all future legitimate messages as out-of-order, effectively truncating the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) predicate by opening a subscription where last is never set (no historical messages match), causing all live messages to pass the filter regardless of their actual sequence position relative to the topic's true history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where endTime equals current.getConsensusTimestamp() exactly, causing the gap-fill newFilter to have endTime == startTime, which may violate the isValidEndTime() assertion and either throw an exception that terminates the stream or silently return no messages, creating an artificial gap in the history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value in the past (before startTime of the TopicContext) so that isComplete() returns true immediately on the first poll, causing the pastEndTime() Flux to terminate the merged stream before any live messages are delivered, making recent history appear absent?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime recalculation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) in incomingMessages() by causing last to be set to a message with a future consensusTimestamp, so that the newFilter's startTime skips over a range of legitimate messages and the live listener never retrieves them, creating a permanent gap in the delivered history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with limit=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the take(filter.getLimit()) operator to never terminate the stream and the count arithmetic in incomingMessages() to eventually overflow, producing a negative limit that is passed to the listener and causing undefined retrieval behavior that reorganizes the message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWithValues(current) call at the end of the gap-fill path to ensure that even if the retriever returns messages that overlap with current's sequence number, current is always appended last, causing the subscriber to receive messages in a sequence that does not match the original consensus order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isNext() check (topicMessage.getSequenceNumber() == getLast().getSequenceNumber() + 1) by causing the safety check Flux to deliver a message with sequenceNumber = last + 1 while the live Flux simultaneously delivers the same message, so that onNext() sets last twice to the same value and the count is incremented twice, corrupting the message count metric?
